human:
POINTS:
- [rubix-bookkeeper/src/main/java/com/qubole/rubix/bookkeeper/LocalDataTransferServer.java:184] Thread creation is fast and we're going through an idle timeout on client side anyway before the thread on server is released. So keeping this at 1m for simplicity.
v1:
SUMMARY:
PR bounds the LocalDataTransferServer thread pool (was unbounded `newCachedThreadPool`) and changes `DEFAULT_SERVER_MAX_THREADS` from `Integer.MAX_VALUE` to `4096`, which also bounds the already-configured BookKeeperServer. Good motivation, but the thread pool lifecycle is broken and the rejection behavior under load needs attention.

POINTS:
- [rubix-bookkeeper/src/main/java/com/qubole/rubix/bookkeeper/LocalDataTransferServer.java:182] **Thread pool is a local variable — cannot be shutdown.** The `ExecutorService` is created inside `run()` but `stop()` (line ~213) only closes `listener`. The thread pool should be a class-level field so `stop()` can call `threadPool.shutdown()` and `threadPool.awaitTermination()`. Currently, on server stop, in-flight client tasks are abandoned and the thread pool is orphaned until GC.

- [rubix-bookkeeper/src/main/java/com/qubole/rubix/bookkeeper/LocalDataTransferServer.java:186] **SynchronousQueue will reject tasks when pool is full — no rejection policy specified.** With `corePoolSize=0`, `maxPoolSize=4096`, and `SynchronousQueue`, once 4096 threads are busy, new tasks will be rejected with `RejectedExecutionException` (default `AbortPolicy`). Under high concurrent load this will cause client connections to fail silently (the exception is swallowed by the `catch (IOException e)` block). Consider using a bounded `LinkedBlockingQueue` instead (e.g., `new LinkedBlockingQueue<>(1000)`) so excess tasks queue rather than fail, or add an explicit `RejectedExecutionHandler`.

- [rubix-spi/src/main/java/com/qubole/rubix/spi/CommonUtilities.java:92] **ThreadFactory does not set daemon threads — inconsistent with rest of codebase.** Other ThreadFactory implementations in the codebase (`Hadoop2ClusterManager.java:57`, `CachingInputStream.java:68`) explicitly call `t.setDaemon(true)`. The new `threadsNamed()` utility should support setting daemon status, or `LocalDataTransferServer` should ensure its worker threads are daemon threads to avoid blocking JVM shutdown.

- [rubix-spi/src/main/java/com/qubole/rubix/spi/CacheConfig.java:72] **Config key naming is inconsistent — may confuse users.** New key `KEY_LOCAL_SERVER_MAX_THREADS` is `"rubix.local.transfer.max-threads"`, but the default value `DEFAULT_SERVER_MAX_THREADS` is shared with the existing `getServerMaxThreads()` whose key is `"rubix.cache.bookkeeper.max-threads"` (line ~125). Users may not realize that `rubix.local.transfer.max-threads` and `rubix.cache.bookkeeper.max-threads` both default to the same value but are independent configs. Consider documenting this relationship or using a shared default constant name that reflects its broader scope.

- [rubix-bookkeeper/src/main/java/com/qubole/rubix/bookkeeper/LocalDataTransferServer.java:180] **Minor: `getServerMaxThreads` is imported but never used in this file.** The import at line 49 is unused since the PR uses `getLocalTransferServerMaxThreads` instead. Clean up the unnecessary import.
v2:
SUMMARY:
This PR replaces an unbounded `Executors.newCachedThreadPool()` in `LocalDataTransferServer` with a configurable `ThreadPoolExecutor`, changes the default max threads for `BookKeeperServer` from `Integer.MAX_VALUE` to `4096`, and adds a `threadsNamed()` utility. The direction is sound, but there is a resource leak and an unused import.

POINTS:
- [rubix-bookkeeper/src/main/java/com/qubole/rubix/bookkeeper/LocalDataTransferServer.java:182] **ThreadPoolExecutor is never shut down.** The `threadPool` is created inside `run()` but when `LocalServer.stop()` is called, only `listener.close()` is executed. The executor service and its threads are leaked. Add a field to hold the `ExecutorService`, and call `threadPool.shutdown()` (or `shutdownNow()`) in the `stop()` method. Also consider calling `threadPool.awaitTermination(...)` for graceful shutdown.

- [rubix-bookkeeper/src/main/java/com/qubole/rubix/bookkeeper/LocalDataTransferServer.java:53] **Unused import.** `getServerMaxThreads` is statically imported but never referenced in this file — only `getLocalTransferServerMaxThreads` is used. Remove the unused import to keep the code clean.

- [rubix-spi/src/main/java/com/qubole/rubix/spi/CacheConfig.java:333] **Misleading default constant name.** Method `getLocalTransferServerMaxThreads()` uses `DEFAULT_SERVER_MAX_THREADS` as its fallback default, but that constant name semantically belongs to the BookKeeper server (key `rubix.cache.bookkeeper.max-threads`). Consider introducing `DEFAULT_LOCAL_SERVER_MAX_THREADS` for clarity, or at minimum add a comment explaining why the same default is shared.

- [rubix-bookkeeper/src/main/java/com/qubole/rubix/bookkeeper/LocalDataTransferServer.java:185] **Core pool size of 0 with SynchronousQueue.** Using core=0 with `SynchronousQueue` means threads are created on-demand and reused only if idle within 60s. This behaves like `newCachedThreadPool` but with a bounded max. If the intent is to keep a minimum pool of threads alive (avoiding thread creation overhead for sustained load), consider a non-zero core pool size (e.g., matching `Runtime.getRuntime().availableProcessors()` or a small fixed number).

- [rubix-spi/src/main/java/com/qubole/rubix/spi/CommonUtilities.java:87] **Minor: unused import.** The diff adds `import static java.util.concurrent.Executors.defaultThreadFactory;` but `defaultThreadFactory` is not used — `ThreadFactoryBuilder` uses its own defaults. Remove the unused import.
v3:
SUMMARY:
This PR replaces `Executors.newCachedThreadPool()` with a bounded `ThreadPoolExecutor` in `LocalDataTransferServer` and changes the default max threads from `Integer.MAX_VALUE` to `4096` for both LocalDataTransferServer and BookKeeperServer.

POINTS:
- [rubix-bookkeeper/src/main/java/com/qubole/rubix/bookkeeper/LocalDataTransferServer.java:52] **Unused import**: `getServerMaxThreads` is imported but never used. Only `getLocalTransferServerMaxThreads` is called (line 183). Remove the unused import to avoid confusion.

- [rubix-bookkeeper/src/main/java/com/qubole/rubix/bookkeeper/LocalDataTransferServer.java:182-186] **Missing `RejectedExecutionHandler`**: The `ThreadPoolExecutor` uses a `SynchronousQueue` with a max pool size of 4096 but no rejection policy. When all 4096 threads are busy, new tasks will trigger the default `AbortPolicy`, throwing `RejectedExecutionException`. This exception is **not caught** by the `run()` loop (which catches only `AsynchronousCloseException` and `IOException`), so a connection burst exceeding the thread limit will crash the server thread. Add a rejection handler (e.g., `CallerRunsPolicy` or a custom handler that logs and drops gracefully) or add a catch block for `RejectedExecutionException`.

- [rubix-bookkeeper/src/main/java/com/qubole/rubix/bookkeeper/LocalDataTransferServer.java:182-186] **Thread pool is never shut down**: The `ExecutorService` is created inside `run()` and is scoped to the method — it is never shut down when `stop()` is called. When the listener is closed and the `run()` loop exits (via `AsynchronousCloseException`), the thread pool is abandoned and its threads leak. Consider storing the `ExecutorService` as a field on `LocalServer` and calling `threadPool.shutdown()` in the `stop()` method or in a `finally` block.

- [rubix-spi/src/main/java/com/qubole/rubix/spi/CacheConfig.java:160] **Constant `DEFAULT_SERVER_MAX_THREADS` is shared across two servers**: This constant now serves as the default for both `getServerMaxThreads()` (BookKeeperServer) and `getLocalTransferServerMaxThreads()` (LocalDataTransferServer). Consider renaming to `DEFAULT_MAX_THREADS` or introducing a separate `DEFAULT_LOCAL_TRANSFER_MAX_THREADS` to avoid semantic confusion.

- [rubix-bookkeeper/src/main/java/com/qubole/rubix/bookkeeper/LocalDataTransferServer.java:182-186] **`corePoolSize=0` with `SynchronousQueue` is unconventional**: A core pool size of 0 means threads are not retained when idle — any idle thread is terminated after 60 seconds. Combined with `SynchronousQueue`, this means every new connection potentially spawns a new thread (up to 4096). If sustained concurrency is expected, consider a larger `corePoolSize` (e.g., `Runtime.getRuntime().availableProcessors()`) with a bounded `LinkedBlockingQueue` instead, to avoid repeated thread creation/teardown overhead.
v4:
SUMMARY:
This PR replaces an unbounded cached thread pool in LocalDataTransferServer with a bounded ThreadPoolExecutor and adds config support. However, the thread pool is never shut down (resource leak), the SynchronousQueue + AbortPolicy combination risks abrupt task rejection under load, and changing DEFAULT_SERVER_MAX_THREADS from Integer.MAX_VALUE silently affects BookKeeperServer too.

POINTS:
- [rubix-bookkeeper/LocalDataTransferServer.java:182] **Thread pool is never shut down — resource leak.** The `threadPool` is a local variable inside `run()`. Method `stop()` (line ~200) only closes `listener` but never calls `threadPool.shutdown()` or `shutdownNow()`. Under high traffic, the 4096 worker threads will persist indefinitely after server stop. Make `threadPool` a field on `LocalServer` and invoke `threadPool.shutdown()` in `stop()`.

- [rubix-bookkeeper/LocalDataTransferServer.java:182-186] **SynchronousQueue + default AbortPolicy causes abrupt task rejection.** `SynchronousQueue` has zero capacity — handoff succeeds only if a thread is immediately available. When all 4096 threads are busy, the default `AbortPolicy` throws `RejectedExecutionException`, which is swallowed by the outer `catch (IOException)` and never logged. Under a load spike, client connections will silently fail. Consider using a bounded `LinkedBlockingQueue` (e.g. capacity = maxThreads) to queue excess tasks, or set a `CallerRunsPolicy` so the accepting thread itself processes the overflow.

- [rubix-spi/CacheConfig.java:161] **Changing DEFAULT_SERVER_MAX_THREADS from Integer.MAX_VALUE to 4096 silently affects BookKeeperServer.** BookKeeperServer (BookKeeperServer.java:141) already calls `getServerMaxThreads(conf)`, which reads this default. The PR title claims both servers are addressed, but BookKeeperServer.java is not modified — yet its behavior changes from unbounded to capped at 4096 Thrift worker threads. This is a breaking behavioral change for BookKeeperServer that could cause Thrift request rejection under extreme load. Document this impact or scope the default change to only the new config key.

- [rubix-spi/CacheConfig.java:333] **getLocalTransferServerMaxThreads reuses DEFAULT_SERVER_MAX_THREADS as its fallback default.** The new config key `KEY_LOCAL_SERVER_MAX_THREADS` falls back to `DEFAULT_SERVER_MAX_THREADS` (4096). This tightly couples two semantically distinct configs (local transfer server vs. BookKeeper server). Consider giving the new config its own explicit default constant (e.g. `DEFAULT_LOCAL_TRANSFER_MAX_THREADS`) to keep defaults independent and make intent clear.

- [rubix-bookkeeper/LocalDataTransferServer.java:182-186] **Core pool size of 0 means threads are always subject to keep-alive timeout.** With `corePoolSize=0` and `keepAliveTime=60s`, even lightly-loaded servers will continuously create and destroy threads as connections arrive/depart. If the server sustains moderate throughput, this causes unnecessary thread churn. Consider setting `corePoolSize` to a small fixed value (e.g. `Runtime.getRuntime().availableProcessors()`).
